commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Consumed. Chap. xliv. 27. No country shall afford them protection. (Calmet)
The richer sort were made captives, and most of the people destroyed, but not all, chap. iv. 27. (Worthington)

commentaries.author: Patrick of Ireland
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh I will scatter them also among the nations, whom neither they nor their fathers have known: and I will send a sword after them, till I have consumed them. Patrick of Ireland I was then about sixteen years of age. I did not know the true God. I was taken as a captive to Ireland with many thousands of people, and deservedly so, because we had turned away from God and did not keep his commandments and did not obey our priests, who used to remind us of our salvation. The Lord brought over us the wrath of his anger and scattered us among many nations, even unto the utmost part of the earth, where now my insignificance is placed among strangers.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
